turn-1-full.text: What was the word for 'it', 'him', or 'you' (formal)?

turn-3-reveal.text: Lo. To make it feminine, change the 'o' to 'a', giving us la. This means 'her', 'it' (for feminine nouns), or 'you' (formal, feminine). For example, 'casa' (house) is feminine.

turn-3-prompt.text: How would you say 'I want to see it', referring to the house?

turn-5-reveal.text: This could also mean 'I want to see her'. 'La' could mean 'her', 'it', or 'you' (formal, feminine). The use of formal language varies by region, so it's safe to start formally with 'lo' or 'la'. 'Quiere' means 'he wants', 'she wants', 'it wants', or 'you (formal) want'.

turn-5-prompt.text: How would you say 'you want to visit me'?

turn-7-reveal.text: To make it a question, just change your intonation.

turn-7-prompt.text: How would you say 'Do you want to visit me?'

turn-9-prompt.text: How would you say 'You don't want to visit me'?

turn-11-prompt.text: how would you ask 'Don't you want to visit me?'

turn-13-prompt.text: How would you ask 'Why don't you want to visit me?'

turn-15-prompt.text: How would you say 'Do you want to come?'

turn-17-prompt.text: How would you say 'Do you want to come to the house?'

turn-19-prompt.text: ¿Quiere venir a la casa? How about 'Do you want to come to visit me?'

turn-21-prompt.text: ¿Quiere venir a visitarme? Now, 'Don't you want to come to visit me?'

turn-23-prompt.text: How would you ask 'Why don't you want to come to visit me?'

turn-25-reveal.text: The word 'debo' means 'I owe' or 'I must'.

turn-25-prompt.text: How would you say 'I must continue'?

turn-27-prompt.text: How would you say 'I must identify it'?

turn-29-prompt.text: For feminine objects, what change would you make?

outro-1-full.text: You've learned how to use 'lo' and 'la' for direct objects and formal address in Spanish.

outro-2-full.text: Try forming questions using 'quieres' and 'por qué'. Practice with different verbs and objects.

outro-3-full.text: Remember, intonation is key for forming questions in Spanish. Practice changing statements into questions.
